title: Privacy Policy
subtitle: How Performance Interpreting collects, uses and protects your personal data.
metaDescription: Privacy policy for Performance Interpreting Ltd. Learn how we collect, use, store and protect your personal data in accordance with UK GDPR and the Data Protection Act 2018.

sections.heading: Who we are
body: Performance Interpreting Ltd ('we', 'us', 'our') is a private limited company registered in England and Wales (Company No. 10684652). Our registered office is Onega House, 112 Main Road, Sidcup, DA14 6NE. We provide BSL and ISL interpreting services for live events across the United Kingdom and Ireland.

We are the data controller for the personal data described in this policy. We are registered with the Information Commissioner's Office (ICO registration reference: ZA912087). You can contact us at: admin@performanceinterpreting.co.uk.
This policy applies to data collected via our website (performanceinterpreting.co.uk), our events app and through our direct business communications.

sections.heading: What data we collect
body: We collect and process the following categories of personal data:

items.title: Contact and identity data
items.description: Name, email address, phone number and job title - collected when you contact us, submit an enquiry form, or engage us for services.

items.title: Event and transaction data
items.description: Details of events you're organising or attending, booking records and correspondence related to our services.

items.title: Professional data (interpreters)
items.description: NRCPD registration number, qualifications, professional experience and availability - collected when you apply to work with us.

items.title: Usage data
items.description: Technical data about how you use our website and app, including IP address, browser type, pages visited and device information - collected via cookies and analytics tools.

items.title: Communications data
items.description: Records of emails, form submissions and other correspondence you send to us.

sections.heading: How we use your data
body: We use your personal data for the following purposes, relying on the lawful bases indicated:

items.title: Delivering our services (Contract)
items.description: To fulfil bookings, coordinate interpreters for your event and communicate with you about the services you've engaged us for.

items.title: Responding to enquiries (Legitimate interests)
items.description: To respond to contact form submissions, email enquiries and access requests from Deaf community members.

items.title: Interpreter recruitment and management (Contract / Legitimate interests)
items.description: To assess applications from interpreters, manage working relationships and maintain our interpreter pool.

items.title: Legal compliance (Legal obligation)
items.description: To maintain records as required by UK law, including tax and employment records where applicable.

items.title: Marketing communications (Consent or Legitimate interests)
items.description: To send you updates about our services where you have provided consent, or where we have a legitimate interest based on an existing business relationship. You can opt out at any time.

items.title: Website and app improvement (Legitimate interests)
items.description: To understand how our platforms are used and to improve them.

sections.heading: How long we keep your data
body: We retain personal data only for as long as necessary for the purposes for which it was collected, or as required by law.
Enquiry and contact data: 2 years from last contact, unless a service relationship is established.
Client and service data: 7 years from the end of the service relationship, for legal and tax compliance.
Interpreter application data: 1 year if unsuccessful; for the duration of the working relationship plus 7 years if a working relationship is established.
Website analytics data: Up to 26 months, as aggregated or anonymised data where possible.

sections.heading: Sharing your data
body: We do not sell your personal data. We share it only where necessary:
With interpreters and production staff - where needed to deliver services you have engaged us for (e.g., sharing event details with the interpreter team).
With technology providers - cloud hosting, email, CRM and analytics tools used to operate our business, all bound by data processing agreements.
With legal and regulatory authorities - where required by law or to protect our legitimate interests.
We do not transfer personal data outside the UK or EEA without appropriate safeguards.

sections.heading: Your rights
body: Under UK GDPR, you have the following rights regarding your personal data:
Right of access: Request a copy of the personal data we hold about you.
Right to rectification: Ask us to correct inaccurate or incomplete data.
Right to erasure: Request deletion of your personal data, subject to legal retention requirements.
Right to restrict processing: Ask us to limit how we use your data in certain circumstances.
Right to data portability: Receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format.
Right to object: Object to processing based on legitimate interests, including direct marketing.
Right to withdraw consent: Where processing is based on consent, withdraw it at any time without affecting prior processing.

To exercise any of these rights, contact us at admin@performanceinterpreting.co.uk. We will respond within one month. If you believe we have not handled your data correctly, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) at ico.org.uk.

sections.heading: Cookies
body: Our website uses cookies to ensure it functions correctly and to understand how it is used. We use:
Essential cookies: Required for the website to function. Cannot be disabled.

Analytics cookies: Used to understand how visitors use our site (e.g., pages visited, time on site). We use privacy-respecting analytics and do not share this data with third-party advertising platforms.
You can control cookie settings through your browser. Blocking all cookies may affect the functionality of our website.

sections.heading: Changes to this policy
body: We may update this privacy policy from time to time. The current version will always be available at performanceinterpreting.co.uk/privacy. Material changes will be communicated where we hold your contact details.
This policy was last updated in March 2026.
